ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Buldak carbonara ramen; closest_parent 'instant noodle food product' (FOODON_00002473) used as the nearest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A039V (Instant noodles) is the closest defensible parent; no specific code exists for Buldak carbonara flavour variant.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for this specific instant noodle product.

serving.description: 1 packet (130 g dry weight, approximately 280 g prepared)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one packet of Buldak Hot Chicken Carbonara Ramen, approximately 130 g dry weight, yielding roughly 280 g when prepared with the included sauce sachets.

nutrient_source.title: Samyang Buldak Hot Chicken Flavor Ramen Carbonara — Nutrition Facts Panel (USDA Branded Food)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values derived from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Samyang Buldak Hot Chicken Flavor Ramen Carbonara (FDC ID 2346792 and corroborating entries). Values are expressed per 100 g dry product as reported on the nutrition facts panel. Minor rounding applied where multiple branded entries were cross-referenced for consistency.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Buldak Carbonara Ramen specifically. GI of 52 is estimated from the closest analogous food: instant noodles (wheat-based), which are reported at GI 52 (±5) in Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. This is consistent with the University of Sydney GI Database entry for instant noodles (GI ~47–52). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 89.96 g carbohydrate in 130 g serving) / 100 ≈ 37.4. Carbohydrate per serving = 69.2 g/100 g × 130 g = 89.96 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content (1077 mg per 100 g; approximately 1400 mg per standard 130 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Buldak Carbonara Ramen due to its very high sodium content of approximately 1400 mg per serving, which represents over 60% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg and can significantly elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate load with a glycemic load of approximately 37 per serving, derived from wheat-based instant noodles
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume Buldak Carbonara Ramen only occasionally and in reduced portions, as the high glycemic load (~37 per standard serving) from refined wheat noodles can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; pairing with non-starchy vegetables and a protein source may partially attenuate the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (1077 mg/100 g), elevated saturated fat (7.7 g/100 g), and ultra-processed formulation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of Buldak Carbonara Ramen due to its combination of very high sodium, elevated saturated fat content, and ultra-processed ingredient profile, all of which are independently associated with increased cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Buldak Hot Chicken Carbonara Ramen is an ultra-processed instant noodle product manufactured by Samyang Foods of South Korea. It belongs to the popular Buldak (fire chicken) series, which is internationally recognised for its intense spiciness. The Carbonara variant distinguishes itself by combining the signature fiery chilli-based sauce with a creamy, dairy-inflected carbonara-style sauce, producing a flavour profile that is simultaneously spicy and rich. The noodles are thick, chewy wheat-based strands that are boiled, drained, and then tossed with the included liquid carbonara sauce sachet and a dry topping sachet containing sesame seeds and dried vegetables. The result is a glossy, cream-coated noodle dish with a moderate-to-high spice level (approximately 6 out of 10 on a consumer heat scale). Per 100 g dry weight, the product provides approximately 465 kcal, 10.8 g protein, 16.9 g fat (of which 7.7 g is saturated), and 69.2 g carbohydrate, with a notably high sodium content of 1077 mg. The glycaemic index is estimated at 52 (low GI range) based on analogous instant wheat noodle data, but the glycaemic load per standard 130 g serving is high at approximately 37, making portion control important for metabolic health. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those avoiding gluten or dairy. It is widely available in Singapore through food delivery platforms and Korean-cuisine restaurants. Clinically, it is a high-sodium, high-carbohydrate, ultra-processed food that warrants caution for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Buldak Carbonara Ramen?
How to cook Buldak Carbonara Ramen?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are sourced from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entries for Samyang Buldak Carbonara Ramen, which is an exact product match. Confidence is set to medium rather than high because the USDA Branded Foods database is a secondary global source (not a primary regional source such as HPB Singapore Foodo), and minor variation exists across different production batches and regional formulations. GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed literature rather than a direct measurement of this specific product.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are expressed per 100 g dry product weight as labelled. Prepared weight per serving is approximately 280 g. 2. Monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are -1 as these are not reported on the standard nutrition facts panel for this product in the USDA Branded Foods database. 3. Potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all -1 as these micronutrients are not reported in the available branded nutrition facts panel entries for this product. 4. Trans_fat_g is 0.0 and cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as explicitly reported on the product nutrition facts panel (confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods). 5. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate based on instant wheat noodles from Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature. 6. The food_group has been reclassified from the input value of 'Meat and alternatives' to 'Grains and staples' as the primary macronutrient and ingredient is wheat-based noodles, not meat. 7. Halal status is not confirmed for this product as sold in Singapore; consumers should verify with the restaurant or supplier. 8. The cuisine tag 'The Noodle Place - Circular Road' from the input has been preserved in the source metadata but is a restaurant name, not a cuisine type; cuisine has been correctly set to Korean.
